cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks. The options are A to D. First, I need to look at the relevant part of the text where Isabelle talks about her experience. The key quotes are: "I was scared to look at myself, but when I did it was already marvelous and I couldn't believe it. I thought it would be blue and swollen but it was already beautiful." Also, later she says, "I have been saved... It's a miracle somehow." Looking at the options: A) She was too shocked to see herself in the mirror. But the text says she was scared but then thought it was marvelous. So she did look, and it wasn't too shocking. So A is probably not correct. B) She was satisfied with the result. The words "marvelous," "couldn't believe it," "beautiful," and "saved" suggest she's happy. So B seems likely. C) She thought her face couldn't be repaired. The text says doctors gave her little hope, but the question is about her own remarks. She didn't say that; she said when she saw the result, it was good. So C is probably not correct. D) She looked more beautiful than before. She said
